title: English legal system and its context
summary: 9084 — sources of law, common law vs civil law, and structure of English law.
learningObjectives: Outline sources of English law: legislation, case law, EU retained law.
Distinguish common law and civil law traditions.
Explain criminal vs civil law distinctions.
Evaluate rule of law and separation of powers.

simpleExplanation.title: How English law works
simpleExplanation.summary: English law combines Acts of Parliament with judge-made precedent — criminal law punishes wrongs against the state, civil law resolves disputes between parties.
steps: Case law — judicial decisions bind lower courts.
Criminal vs civil — state prosecution vs private claim.

sections.content: **English legal system** (1.1.1) opens 9084 Paper 1. Define **sources** before discussing institutions.
